2026-04-20T11:45:03.1125483Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T11:45:03.1126341Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T11:45:03.1126718Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T11:45:03.1127214Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T11:45:03.1127446Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-20T11:48:21.5829081Z 
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5830413Z ◆ The README.md at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` is for the TypeSpec emitter package. It documents emitter options but does NOT mention the new scaffolding behavior added by this PR.
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5831761Z   
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5832108Z   The new feature added by PR #57653 is:
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5833386Z   1. **NewAzureProjectScaffolding** now auto-generates `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and `Directory.Build.props` when scaffolding a new project under the `sdk/` directory
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5835018Z   2. These files are only created if they don't already exist (preventing overwriting of customized content)
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5836219Z   3. This behavior is gated to the `sdk/` directory
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5836762Z   
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5837015Z   
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5839510Z   4. The `new-project` option already exists in the README but doesn't document that it also triggers generation of these scaffolding files, or that this only happens under `sdk/`, or that users can customize these files without them being overwritten on regeneration—a significant gap for users creating new packages.
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5841624Z   
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5842293Z   Let me check the CONTRIBUTING.md to see if there's any documentation about this scaffolding behavior there.
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5843065Z 
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5844360Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, path: eng/packages/http-client-csharp/CONTRIBU..., ref: main, repo: azure-sdk-for-net)
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5845458Z 
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5846215Z ◆ Looking at the CONTRIBUTING.md, there's no mention of the new scaffolding behavior.
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5846947Z   
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5847976Z   Now let me look at the README.md for the generator more carefully. The README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` currently:
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5849300Z   1. Has a `new-project` option that says "Set to `true` to overwrite the csproj if it already exists"
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5850631Z   2. But doesn't mention the auto-generation of README.md, CHANGELOG.md, and Directory.Build.props when the output is under `sdk/`
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5852342Z   3. No explanation of the scaffolding behavior — what files get generated, when, and that they're not overwritten on regeneration
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5853318Z   
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5853602Z   
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5855401Z   4. The `new-project` option description is incomplete — it should clarify that this also triggers generation of those additional scaffolding files under `sdk/`, and that these files are only created once to preserve customizations
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5857272Z   
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5857551Z   .
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5857804Z   
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5860765Z   Actually, I need to reconsider. The `WriteAdditionalFiles` override in `NewAzureProjectScaffolding.cs` generates those extra files during any project generation, not specifically tied to the `new-project` option. The README's description of `new-project` is just about overwriting the `.csproj` file, so the documentation gap is that there's no mention anywhere of what `WriteAdditionalFiles` does or when those additional scaffolding files get created.
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5863643Z 
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5864739Z ◆ The README.md at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` doesn't mention the new scaffolding behavior. Let me file a focused documentation gap issue.
2026-04-20T11:48:21.5866436Z

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running.
